results.title: Men without faces
results.creator_credit: Dennis A. Hill and New South Wales. Dept. of Prisons
results.brief_description: This film describes the NSW penal system and explains the efforts made by prison officers to rehabilitate prisoners. Shows how hardened prisoners enter Grafton Gaol, a prison of bars and locks, while those who show promise of reforming are sent to training centres in Goulbourn, Berrima and Emu Plains where they are taught trades and how to live in a community, preparation for the day of their release.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Dennis A. Hill and New South Wales. Dept. of Prisons

results.title: Luanda–Kinshasa
results.credit_line: Courtesy of the artist, Victoria Miro and David Zwirner
results.description:
A band is in the middle of a jam session. Keyboards, guitars, drums, percussion and saxophone lock into a layered rhythm that loops, builds and shifts. A sound engineer listens from the booth while friends, journalists and record label staff watch.

The scene unfolds inside a reconstruction of Columbia Records’ legendary New York studio, known as The Church. In the 1960s and 70s it hosted landmark recordings by Bob Dylan, Pink Floyd and Miles Davis. The musicians draw on African diasporic music that was moving through New York at the time, including Afrobeat songs like O Boso by Manu Dibango. They blend jazz, rock and African rhythms into something new.

Canadian artist Stan Douglas constructs a speculative history: crafting a six-hour remix by sampling  two compositions, ‘Luanda’ and ‘Kinshasa’. Through editing and synchronisation, musicians appear to collaborate across time and space, showing how sound and technology can reorganise history into new cultural possibilities.

results.title: Finding the way home
results.creator_credit: Julie McDonald and Children's Welfare Association of Victoria
results.brief_description: Foster parents in rural Victoria discuss what it’s like to care for a foster child, covering issues such as selecting a child, financial implications, emotional support, and most importantly, the benefits. Families interviewed include a single mother with children, an older childless couple, and a middle aged couple with their own children. A young man whose lived with a foster family also shares his experience. Worthwhile viewing for anyone considering fostering a child. Supported by the Federal Department of Family and Community Services.

results.title: For love or money: a history of women and work in Australia
results.creator_credit: Jeni Thornley, Megan McMurchy, Margot Nash, Margot Oliver and Flashback Films
results.brief_description: Tells the story of women’s working lives throughout Australia’s history. Juxtaposes clips from feature films, home movies, newsreels and documentaries with radio shows, diaries, popular songs, letters and interviews. Explores the challenges that face women in the nuclear age. Narrated by Noni Hazlehurst; edited by Margot Nash; music by Elizabeth Drake.

results.title: Tit for tat [Censored version]
results.creator_credit: Martha Ansara, Graeme Isaac, Film Australia and Mayfan
results.brief_description: Examines the world of tattoos and the surrounding culture of bikers and rebels as the filmmaker travels with Baldy, a biker and photo-journalist, to the Sandhurst Rally for the yearly Tattoo Exhibition and judging. There they meet AJ, the most tattooed man in Australia and a renowned tattoo artist himself. WARNING - SOME SCENES IN THIS PROGRAMME MAY OFFEND SOME VIEWERS.

results.title: Seeing red and feeling blue
results.creator_credit: Jane Oehr, Suzanne Baker and Film Australia
results.brief_description: Provides basic information about reproduction and aspects of menstruation, and probes emotional responses on many aspects of womanhood. The Women’s Theatre Group of Melbourne performs improvisations and satirical sketches which comment on many of society’s prevailing attitudes towards sex education and women.

results.title: They reckon a woman's world's just it and a bit
results.creator_credit: Meg Stewart, Penny Chapman and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Five housewives, living in a housing estate in outer Adelaide, tell how they organized a mutual self-help group to overcome the boredom, frustration and loneliness of living in suburbia.

results.title: Woolloomooloo
results.brief_description: A social and historical documentary on the redevelopment of one of Sydney’s oldest harbourside areas, from the initial plans of 1969 to the completion of the Housing Commission’s renovation project in late 1977. Details the roles of local, State and Federal governments, the State Planning Authority, The National Trust, the developers, the Builders’ Labourers Federation led by Jack Mundey, and the residents groups.

results.title: Farewell to charms
results.creator_credit: Carla Pontiac, Carole Sklan and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: An experimental feminist film which questions the goals of love and romance as a woman’s ultimate fulfillment. Emma, vivacious but suicidal terrorist is reunited with Cecily, her old school chum. They meet a ‘bionic bike dyke’ called stretch and break open the jelly beans. Will they stretch a point over the pool table? Will tap-dancing and vaginal exercises be enough for Cecily? Will Emma metamorphose into Esther Williams? True romance. Cast includes Radda Jordan, Helen Pankhurst.

results.title: The Moonage daydreams of Charlene Stardust
results.creator_credit: Digby Duncan, Jan Carroll, Jan Milne, Margot Oliver and Women's Film Workshop
results.brief_description: Charlene Stardust dreams of her fantasy date in anticipation of her approaching weekend date. As the time, approaches Charlene has become disenchanted by the conventional male and female roles and realises it’s a change, not marriage, that she really needs.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Digby Duncan, Jan Carroll, Jan Milne, Margot Oliver and Women’s Film Workshop
